label: William Friedman vs Mercer Summer Intern Analyst

profile.profile_text:
Name: Test Account PG100

Job type: Current student

Target roles: consulting internships, strategy consulting

Target locations: NYC, Chicago, Boston

Preferred locations: NYC, Chicago, Boston

Timeline: Summer 2026

Resume:
William Friedman
wfried@umich.edu | (561) 866-0780 | www.linkedin.com/in/william--friedman 

Analytical economics student who brings experience in strategy, financial modeling, and data driven problem solving across private investments and consulting exposure programs. Proven ability to break down ambiguous questions, conduct rigorous quantitative analysis, and communicate insights to senior stakeholders.


EDUCATION

University of Michigan, College of Literature, Science, and the Arts 			                            Ann Arbor, MI
Bachelor of Arts in Economics with a Minor in Statistics 					        Expected May 2027 
GPA: 3.90    Academic Honors: University Honors (all semesters); James B. Angell Scholar  
Relevant Coursework: Econometrics, Intermediate Micro/Macro Economics, Game Theory, Corporate Finance, Applied Statistics, Statistical Computing, Data Analysis  

EXPERIENCE

Aspen Academies (Private Investment Firm - M&A Strategy Focus) 			                                  Denver, CO
Summer Strategy Analyst 									    May 2025 – Aug 2025 
Supported M&A strategy through market research and due diligence on early childcare targets, assessing student counts, salary structures, and financial performance.
Designed and executed a proprietary data collection approach using “mystery shopping” calls to obtain hard to access operational and financial data, organizing results in Excel.
Led targeted seller outreach, crafting personalized, data driven emails to convert prospects into the diligence pipeline.
Conducted financial analysis including DCF modeling and P&L / Sources and Uses review, presenting investment insights to firm executives.
The Boston Consulting Group (BCG) 							                                  Boston, MA
Launch Program Participant 									     May 2025 – Jun 2025 
Participated in an intensive program to gain exposure to management consulting, BCG's culture, and career development pathways. 
Engaged in workshops and Q&A sessions focused on consulting casework, structured problem-solving, and the core recruiting process. 
Selected Project: Advanced Statistical Modeling 
Applied Linear Regression in R to analyze key variables impacting car prices (horsepower, RPM, weight, body type) 
Conducted quantitative analysis to determine the marginal effect of technical specifications on final market price, culminating in a detailed written report and data visualization.

ADDITIONAL EXPERIENCE

Luxury Retail Sales & Operations  (Alo & Brooks Brothers)
Sales Associate (Full-Time) May – August 2024 (Alo); June – August 2023 (Brooks Brothers)
INVOLVEMENT & LEADERSHIP

University of Michigan’s Men’s Club Ice Hockey					                            Ann Arbor, MI
President, Assistant Captain, Executive Board Member 						        May 2025 – Present 
Directed all operational and strategic aspects for a 30-athlete organization, reporting to three distinct governing bodies (University, ACHA, TSCHL) to ensure full compliance.
Led and executed the hiring process for a new Assistant Coach, including recruitment, interviewing, and selection. 
Managed a 7-member Executive Board and served as primary liaison for prospective players, overseeing outreach and onboarding.
Vice President of Operations and Finance 						                   Sep 2023 – May 2025 
Managed and built an annual operating budget of $150,000 using Google Sheets, allocating funds for travel, uniforms, ice time, coaching, and equipment. 
Successfully secured $1,600 in non-dues funding by applying to the Student Organization Funding Committee (SOFC) and spearheaded an on-campus fundraising day that raised almost $5,000.
Coordinated all game-day logistics for a 30-game season, including scheduling ice time, referees, trainers, and arranging complex travel (vans, buses, hotels) across multiple regions.
SKILLS & INTERESTS

Technical Skills: Microsoft Excel (Advanced), R (Programming Language), SQL, PowerPoint, Google Sheets, Canva.
Interests: Reading (Fantasy/Sci-Fi), Pop Culture, Sports

jobText:
Job description
We are seeking a talented individual to join our Career Consulting team at Mercer as a Summer Intern Analyst. This role will be based in Washington DC. This is a hybrid role that has a requirement of working at least three days a week in the office. Internship program dates are Monday, June 1st – Friday, August 7th, 2026.

Mercer is uniquely qualified to help organizations design and execute sophisticated workforce strategies, whether it’s for a multinational organization or a local company attempting to grow within its market. We provide best-in-class consulting, rigorous analytics, and data-driven insights that help organizations make fact-based decisions.

As a Summer Intern Analyst on Mercer’s Career team, you will support client engagements across industries as an integral member of project teams. You’ll help diagnose client challenges and contribute to the design of strategic talent solutions across Rewards, HR Transformation, Talent Strategy, Workforce Planning & Analytics, and Communications. The role combines hands-on research, modeling, and problem solving with client interaction, working with senior clients and internal experts in a collaborative, learning-oriented environment. You will gain practical exposure to Mercer’s integrated approach to improving business performance through people.

We will count on you to:
• Conduct research and analysis to understand industry and organization-specific issues that could include business strategy, corporate performance, compensation and rewards practices and trends, global expansion, and best practices in talent management
• Collect and examine data relevant to developing solutions tailored to the client’s needs
• Analyze clients' strategic, financial, and organizational information to provide insights for more senior members of the project team
• Participate in team brainstorming sessions to develop client recommendations
• Prepare client presentations, including recommendations to be presented to boards of directors, executives, senior management, and HR leaders

What you need to have:
• Progress toward a bachelor's degree
• Status as a currently enrolled student; candidates should have an expected graduation date between the Spring of 2027 through Spring of 2028
• Strong academic achievement demonstrated through rigorous course work, and the ability to successfully balance extra-curricular activities
• A strong interest in human capital consulting
• Legal work authorization to work in the U.S. on a permanent and ongoing basis without the need for sponsorship now or in the future is required

What makes you stand out?
• Strong analytical skills, both quantitative and qualitative, and working knowledge of Microsoft Office— specifically Excel and PowerPoint
• Knowledge of data analysis, storytelling with data, and presentation design
• Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills
• Intellectual curiosity; seeking opportunities to develop new skills and apply critical thinking and innovative solutions to solve problems
• Ability to collaborate with diverse teams and communicate with others in a skillful, engaging, and proactive manner
• Strong time management skills, with the flexibility to work under tight deadlines and adapt to changing client needs
• Superior organizational skills and strong attention to detail
